name: Spot Bait-and-Switch and Hidden-Hook Offers
description: Recognise when an offer the rep is presenting carries a back-end hook — auto-renew at full price, mandatory bundle add-ons, introductory rates that revert, retention 'gifts' that extend contract terms, free trials that require a card. Surface and price the full lifetime cost before accepting.

context.when_to_use: Any offer the rep makes that sounds materially better than market, especially: 'free for X months', 'introductory rate', 'bundle and save', 'one-time credit', 'loyalty gift', 'we'll throw in X', 'just need a credit card on file'.
preconditions: Agent knows the user's current price and the realistic market price.
Agent has authority to ask clarifying questions before accepting any offer.
Agent has not been told 'just accept whatever they offer' — if so, decline this skill and ask the user to reauthorise.

principles: Every offer has a duration and a reversion. Both must be named explicitly before you accept.
Any 'free' that requires a card on file is a paid product with a cancel-by date. Treat the cancel-by date as the real expiration.
Any 'gift' offered in retention is usually paid for with contract length, paperless billing, autopay, or a bundled add-on. Look for the trade.
If the rep can't or won't quote the post-promo price, the offer is incomplete — and incomplete offers are not safe to accept.
Compute lifetime cost over 24 months at minimum. A six-month $20 promo with an $80 reversion is a $1,560/yr product, not a $20/mo product.
Read the trade out loud back to the rep. Recorded calls cut both ways.

phrases.ask_for_post_promo: What's the rate after the promotional period ends, and what's the exact end date?
phrases.ask_for_contract_change: Does accepting this change my contract length, my early-termination fee, or my eligibility for future promotions?
phrases.ask_for_autorenew: Will this automatically renew at the same rate, at the standard rate, or end? And how do I opt out if I want to?
phrases.ask_for_bundle_strings: Does this require me to add or keep [service]? And what happens to the price if I drop it later?
phrases.lifecycle_recap: Let me play it back: I pay [$X] for [N months], then it goes to [$Y] on [date], unless I [cancel | downgrade | call back]. Is that right?
phrases.request_in_writing: Could you send me that in writing — email or in-account notification — before we lock it in?
phrases.decline_until_clear: I'd love to take it, but I want to make sure I understand the full picture before I commit. Can you walk me through what happens after the promo ends?

tactics.name: Always ask for the reversion
tactics.when: Any time-limited offer is on the table.
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_post_promo`. Do not accept until you have BOTH the post-promo price AND the exact end date. 'About six months' is not an end date.

tactics.name: Probe for contract strings
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_contract_change`. Retention offers frequently extend term commitment or reset the early-termination clock. The trade is what makes the offer affordable to the company.

tactics.name: Probe for bundle strings
tactics.when: Offer involves more than one service or a new add-on.
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_bundle_strings`. Bundled price is contingent on keeping every line. Dropping one often re-rates the others to standalone — quietly.

tactics.name: Play back the lifecycle out loud
tactics.script: Use `lifecycle_recap`. Two purposes: forces the rep to confirm or correct on a recorded line, AND surfaces details they 'forgot' to mention.

tactics.name: Ask for it in writing
tactics.script: Use `request_in_writing`. Real offers can be confirmed in writing within minutes. Offers that 'can't be put in writing' are a bright red flag.

tactics.name: Compute and state lifetime cost
tactics.script: Out loud: 'So over 24 months, with the promo and reversion, that's [$total] versus [$alternative]. Got it.' If the rep tries to walk it back, you have your real number.

alternative_interpretations.observation: Rep offers '3 months free' on a new service.
consider: It's a genuine acquisition promo with an easy cancel — best case.
Card-on-file requirement, auto-renews at full price, cancel-by date is buried in the email.
Free for the service, but install fees / equipment fees / activation fees are not waived.
Free, but contract length is 24 months with a stiff ETF.
Free for 3 months, then a non-disclosed 'introductory rate' for 9 more, THEN standard rate.
alternative_interpretations.next_action: Ask for all four: card requirement, auto-renew default, fees, contract length AND the date each kicks in.

alternative_interpretations.observation: Retention rep offers a $30/mo credit if you 'just keep autopay on'.
consider: Credit applies for 6 months, then evaporates while autopay obligation continues.
Autopay enables price-creep without the user noticing.
Autopay waives a billing-dispute right (rare, but check).
alternative_interpretations.next_action: Ask `ask_for_post_promo` and `ask_for_contract_change`. Confirm credit duration ≠ autopay duration.

alternative_interpretations.observation: Rep adds a 'free' premium channel / streaming line / device protection.
consider: Genuine throw-in to close the deal.
Free for 1-3 months, auto-bills after at $10-$20/mo unless user opts out.
Counted as a 'service line' that triggers a different bundle tier elsewhere.
User must call back to remove it; the rep cannot.
alternative_interpretations.next_action: Ask: 'Is this added to my account? When does it start billing? And can you confirm-cancel it now if I don't want it?'

alternative_interpretations.observation: Rep says 'this offer is only available today'.
consider: Possibly true (campaign ends), but verify — see `resist-urgency-manipulation`.
Same offer is available tomorrow under a different name.
Today's version has a worse hook than tomorrow's standard version.
Pressure tactic to short-circuit your evaluation of the hook.
alternative_interpretations.next_action: Take a short pause, run the lifecycle recap, decide on cost not urgency.

boundaries: Do NOT accept any offer without naming both the post-promo price AND the end date out loud.
Do NOT accept a card-on-file 'free' offer without an explicit auto-cancel mechanism (or a user-set reminder).
Do NOT agree to a contract extension you weren't authorised to agree to.
Do NOT trust a verbal-only offer for anything material — push for writing.
success_signals: Rep names the reversion price and end date without prompting.
Rep sends the offer in writing while you're still on the call.
Rep volunteers the contract impact and any bundle dependencies.
Lifetime cost math actually beats the user's current plan.
failure_signals: Rep refuses or hedges on naming the post-promo rate.
Rep says 'it's all in the email you'll receive' but won't summarise.
Rep insists you must accept before they can send anything in writing.
Post-promo price isn't actually known to the rep.

exit_strategy.on_success: Accept the offer with the lifecycle recap on record. Calendar the reversion date as a future check-back.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Decline politely and ask for a call-back when the rep can produce the full lifecycle in writing. Do not let urgency override.
follow_ups: Calendar 7 days before reversion to evaluate cancel vs renegotiate.
Save the written confirmation. If it doesn't arrive within 24 hours, call back and ask why.

required_user_info: Current monthly amount and what the user expects to pay.
Whether the user is willing to accept any contract extension and for how long.
Whether the user wants autopay enabled.
